startingHypothesis.statement: Trust in AI systems is becoming an evidence problem: durable trust will depend less on stated intent and more on independently repeatable assurance signals across evaluation, monitoring, and operational control.
startingHypothesis.rationale: Multiple fresh sources point toward operational evidence, evaluation, and monitoring as core trust mechanisms.

sourcesRead.title: A Systematic Lifecycle-Referenced Capability Mapping of MLOps Platforms for Energy Forecasting
sourcesRead.summary: Accurate energy forecasting is essential for maintaining power system reliability, integrating renewable generation, and ensuring market stability. Although machine learning has improved forecasting accuracy, its operational deployment depends on Machine Learning Operations (MLOps) platforms that automate and scale the entire lifecycle of energy data pipelines.

sourcesRead.title: AI-Driven Service Intelligence Models for Operational Excellence and Customer Experience Optimization in Service Economies
sourcesRead.summary: Abstract: This manuscript develops a unified academic framework for understanding how artificial intelligence can be integrated into service systems to improve operational excellence and customer experience without collapsing explanation into automation or governance into afterthought. It treats service intelligence as a structured field of inquiry at the intersection of operations, statistics, machine learning, data engineering, and institutional design.

notes.claim: A Systematic Lifecycle-Referenced Capability Mapping of MLOps Platforms for Energy Forecasting adds evidence on assurance measurement.
notes.implication: The trust question should be treated as a testable evidence pipeline, not a static policy claim.
notes.limitation: The metadata-only pass should be followed by closer reading before major confidence changes.

synthesis: Tonight's open-source scan found 2 relevant artifacts clustered around service, energy, forecasting, machine. The working hypothesis remains viable, but the evidence base is still stronger on evaluation and tooling than on independent, repeatable assurance in deployed systems.

hypothesisUpdates.rationale: Multiple fresh sources point toward operational evidence, evaluation, and monitoring as core trust mechanisms.

newHypotheses.statement: Independent AI assurance will converge around continuously refreshed evidence bundles rather than one-time model reports.
Agentic AI trust failures will be easier to detect through workflow telemetry than through benchmark scores alone.

nextSearchPlan: continuous assurance evidence bundles AI systems
agent workflow telemetry trust monitoring evaluation
independent AI audit benchmark operational assurance
unresolvedQuestions: Which assurance signals remain predictive after deployment conditions drift?
What evidence should be independently reproducible versus vendor-attested?
How should agentic workflows expose trust-relevant telemetry without leaking sensitive data?
